features.name: DPI-C import with input parameters called from always block
features.description: Code should include a DPI-C function import with input parameters, followed by a call to that function from within an always @(posedge clk) block using concatenation expressions as arguments
features.snippet:
import "DPI-C" function void sdl_write(input integer y, input integer x, input integer r, input integer g, input integer b);

always @(posedge clk) begin
    if (mem_web) begin
        sdl_write({26'h0,mem_waddr[12:7]}, {25'h0,mem_waddr[6:0]},
            {24'h0,mem_wdata[23:16]}, {24'h0,mem_wdata[15:8]}, {24'h0,mem_wdata[7:0]});
    end
end

features.name: DPI-C import with multiple input parameters
features.description: Code should include a DPI-C import of a void function with five input integer parameters representing coordinates and color values
features.snippet: import "DPI-C" function void sdl_write(input integer y, input integer x, input integer r, input integer g, input integer b);

features.name: DPI function call with concatenation arguments
features.description: Code should include a DPI function call where the arguments use concatenation with zero-extension to create integer values from bit-selects
